content: Hey, I'm thinking of renovating my backyard in Paris. I've got a pretty big garden and I want to make the most of it. I've recently adopted a golden retriever named Willow, so I need to make sure it's dog-friendly.
That sounds like a great project! What kind of renovations are you thinking of doing? And congratulations on the new furry family member!

Well, I was thinking of putting in a pond and a small waterfall. I've always loved the sound of running water. And maybe a little area for Willow to play in. I've got a lot of experience with gardening, so I'm excited to get started.

A pond and waterfall would be beautiful! And a play area for Willow is a great idea. Have you considered what kind of plants you'll put in the garden? You mentioned you have experience with gardening, what's your favorite type of plant to work with?

Yeah, I love working with orchids. I've got a whole collection of them in my greenhouse. But for the backyard, I think I'll stick to more low-maintenance plants. Maybe some succulents and herbs. I've also been getting into mushroom foraging lately, so I might try to incorporate some of those into the garden as well.
Orchids are beautiful! And succulents and herbs are great choices for a low-maintenance garden. Mushroom foraging is a unique hobby, have you found any good spots to forage in Paris?

Actually, I've found a few great spots just outside the city. I've been going out with a friend who's an expert on mushrooms. We went on a foraging trip last weekend and found some amazing chanterelles. I'm thinking of using them in a Thai dish I'm making for my next dinner party.
That sounds like a lot of fun! And delicious! Thai food is one of my favorites. What's your favorite dish to make?
I love making pad thai. I've got a great recipe that I've perfected over the years. And I've also been getting into calligraphy, so I might make some handmade menus for the dinner party.
Pad thai is a classic! And handmade menus with calligraphy will add a nice touch to the dinner party. You're a man of many talents, Finley!

Thanks! I like to stay busy. I've also been working on a new opera piece, so that's been taking up a lot of my time. But I'm hoping to have it finished by the end of the month, so I can perform it at the opera house in Lyon in June.
That's exciting! I'm sure it will be a great performance. You mentioned you're performing in Lyon in June, have you started making travel arrangements yet?
Yeah, I've booked my flights and hotel already. I like to plan ahead. And I'm thinking of taking Willow with me, so I need to make sure the hotel is dog-friendly.
That's great that you're planning ahead! And it's wonderful that you're considering taking Willow with you. I'm sure she'll enjoy the trip.
Definitely. I'm thinking of spending a few extra days in Lyon after the performance, just to explore the city and relax. Maybe do some chess playing in the park.
That sounds like a great plan. Lyon is a beautiful city, and playing chess in the park sounds like a perfect way to unwind. I hope you have a great time, Finley!

Thanks, I'm looking forward to it. I'll be sure to take some photos of the garden and the opera house to share with you when I get back. Maybe I'll even bring back some new crossword puzzles to work on.
I'd love to see them! And I'm sure the crossword puzzles will be a great way to keep your mind sharp. Have a great trip, and don't hesitate to reach out if you need anything!
